meta.title: Quranic Vocabulary
meta.subtitle: High-frequency Quran words with harakat, transliteration, and reading rules per word.

words.ruleNote: alif (alif) — a / glottal stop. Example with fatha: اَلْ
baa (b) — b as in book. Example with fatha: بَ
taa (t) — t as in tea. Example with fatha: تَ
thaa (th) — th as in think. Example with fatha: ثَ
jeem (j) — j as in jam. Example with fatha: جَ
haa (ḥ) — breathy h from throat. Example with fatha: حَ
khaa (kh) — ch in Scottish loch. Example with fatha: خَ
daal (d) — d as in day. Example with fatha: دَ
dhaal (dh) — th as in this. Example with fatha: ذَ
raa (r) — rolled r. Example with fatha: رَ
zaay (z) — z as in zoo. Example with fatha: زَ
seen (s) — s as in sun. Example with fatha: سَ
sheen (sh) — sh as in she. Example with fatha: شَ
saad (ṣ) — emphatic s. Example with fatha: صَ
daad (ḍ) — emphatic d. Example with fatha: ضَ
taa (ṭ) — emphatic t. Example with fatha: طَ
dhaa (ẓ) — emphatic dh. Example with fatha: ظَ
ayn (ʿ) — constriction in throat. Example with fatha: عَ
ghayn (gh) — gargle-like fricative. Example with fatha: غَ
faa (f) — f as in fan. Example with fatha: فَ
qaaf (q) — deep k from back of throat. Example with fatha: قَ
kaaf (k) — k as in kite. Example with fatha: كَ
laam (l) — l as in lamp. Example with fatha: لَ
meem (m) — m as in man. Example with fatha: مَ
noon (n) — n as in no. Example with fatha: نَ
haa (h) — h as in he. Example with fatha: هَ
waaw (w / uu) — w or long uu. Example with fatha: وَ
yaa (y / ii) — y or long ii. Example with fatha: يَ
Fatha (َ) gives short a. Read بَ as "ba".
Kasra (ِ) gives short i. Read بِ as "bi".
Damma (ُ) gives short u. Read بُ as "bu".
Sukun (ْ) gives no vowel. Read بْ as "b".
Shadda (ّ) gives double consonant. Read بّ as "bb".
Tanween Fath (ً) gives an. Read بًا as "ban".
Tanween Kasr (ٍ) gives in. Read بٍ as "bin".
Tanween Damm (ٌ) gives un. Read بٌ as "bun".
Alif Madd (ا after vowel) gives long aa. Read بَا as "baa".
Hamza (ء) gives glottal stop. Read أَ as "a".
Lām in Allāh is heavy after fatḥa; shadda on lām in some scripts.
Fatḥa on rā, then doubled bā (shadda) — hold the b briefly.
ḥ with sukūn, m with fatḥa, dagger alif lengthens ā before nūn.
Kasra on ḥā, long ī from yā (two counts).
Māl- with long ā, -ik with kasra on kāf.
ʿayn then b with sukūn — stop cleanly on b.
Ṣād is emphatic; tā marbūṭa at end = ah in pause.
Za-kaa — long ā before h at pause.
Hamza under alif with kasra when not after waṣl; ā lengthened.
Madd on alif (ā), mīn with kasra + long ī.
Bi-sm — kasra on bā, m with sukūn.
Lām assimilates into ḥā (sun letter); damma on dāl.
ʿayn is moon letter — clear lām; ā then īn ending.
Naʿ-bu-du — ʿayn in middle, damma on bā.
Ṣād is sun letter — lām merges; shadda on ṣād.
An-ʿam — hamza with fatḥa, ʿayn with fatḥa.
Ḍād is sun letter; shadda doubles ḍād.
Qā-la — qāf from deep throat, long ā.
Jā'a — madd on alif before hamza.
Tā merges with tā (shadda) in written form اتّقى.
Kha-la-qa — light qalqalah on qāf with sukūn at stop.
Inna — shadda on nūn, fatḥa on alif.
Il-lā — kasra, doubled lām, long ā.
Min — ikhfa rules apply before certain letters.
Bi — single kasra, connects to next word.
An — nūn sukūn; ikhfa before verbs.
Mā' — hamza at end for glottal stop.
Kāf is sun letter — lām assimilates; kitāb.
Qāf sun letter; Qur-ān with madd on alif.
Idāfa chain — Day of the Standing.
Jīm sun letter; jan-na with shadda.
Nūn sun letter — lām merges into nūn.

words.english: In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Especially Merciful
words.ruleNote: Chain of idāfa: bi-smi Allāhi ar-Raḥmāni ar-Raḥīm. Heavy lām in Allāh after kasra in bi-smi.
Rab-bi — kasra + shadda on bā, then ʿālamīn.

words.english: Master of the Day of Judgment
words.ruleNote: Idāfa: Owner of Day of d-Dīn — dāl is sun letter.
Iyyāka — emphasis on You (kāf object pronoun).

words.english: Guide us to the straight path
words.ruleNote: Duʿā form; ṣād sun letter in aṣ-ṣirāṭ.
Start of Surah al-Ikhlās; aḥad ends with tanwīn fatḥ.
Ṣād sun letter; ṣ-ṣamad with shadda.

words.english: He neither begets nor is born

words.transliteration: wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aḥad
words.english: Nor is there any equivalent to Him
words.ruleNote: Ends Ikhlās; kufuwan aḥad — no equal.
words.context: wa lam yakun lahu kufuwan aḥad

words.arabic: اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ
words.transliteration: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa l-ḥayyu l-qayyūm
words.english: Allah — no god but He, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer
words.context: Allāhu lā ilāha illā huwa l-ḥayyu l-qayyūm

words.ruleNote: Li-llāh — lām assimilates to lām of Allāh.
In shā'a — madd on alif before hamza.

words.english: May Allah reward you with good
